behavior_summary: HEAD::use (Event 1) marks the HEAD object with a specific info key, applies a process-exclude to alter/stop normal processing for that object, and spawns a body-part process (BODYPART::0270) owned by the HEAD object. The spawn return value is captured and returned.

HEAD::hit (Event 5) marks the HEAD object with a different info key, applies process-exclude, invokes a FREE subsystem routine with code 0x0202 (likely a cleanup/state transition), then queries Kernel::getNumProcesses for process type 0x020A for this object. Based on the inverted boolean result of that query it may spawn an alternative body-part process (BODYPART::0431).
notable_control_flow: HEAD::use runs deterministically: set-info -> process-exclude -> spawn BODYPART::0270 -> return.

HEAD::hit contains a conditional branch: after calling FREE::2A56 it calls Kernel::getNumProcesses and applies a logical inversion to the returned count; the inverted test controls whether BODYPART::0431 is spawned. The inversion makes the spawn condition ambiguous from the bytecode (spawn on zero vs non-zero is inferred but uncertain).
open_questions: What semantic meaning do the numeric info/process codes represent (0x0207, 0x020B, 0x0202, 0x020A)? They appear to be metadata or process-type identifiers but their high-level names are unknown.

Does the inverted test in HEAD::hit spawn BODYPART::0431 when Kernel::getNumProcesses returns zero or when it returns non-zero? The bytecode shows a NOT applied to the return value before the conditional, making the exact condition unclear.

What is the in-object 2-byte indirect field (consistently passed to extern calls)? It is used as an identifier/key for the object instance but its exact meaning (instance id, template id, slot index, etc.) is not documented.

main_function.name: U8P_HEAD::use
main_function.purpose: Handle the game's 'U8P_HEAD::use' action for the HEAD object: attach a specific info flag to the object, exclude it from normal processing, and spawn a BODYPART::0270 process linked to that object.
entry_conditions: Event 1 (U8P_HEAD::use) — invoked when the HEAD object is used/activated by the player or another actor.

function_map.name: U8P_HEAD::use
function_map.role: Primary event handler for the HEAD object's 'U8P_HEAD::use' action; updates object state and spawns an associated body-part process.

function_map.name: U8P_HEAD::hit
function_map.role: Event handler for the HEAD object's 'hit' action; updates object state, triggers FREE subsystem behavior, conditionally spawns an alternate body-part process based on existing processes.

function_map.name: U8P_HEAD::set info
function_map.role: Engine primitive to attach/update metadata or state keyed by numeric id on the object.

function_map.name: U8P_HEAD::process exclude
function_map.role: Engine primitive to mark an object/process so it is excluded from or altered in subsequent processing.

function_map.name: spawn (BODYPART::0270)
function_map.role: Spawn/start a body-part process (ID 0x0270) owned by the HEAD object; used by HEAD::use.

function_map.name: spawn (BODYPART::0431)
function_map.role: Spawn/start a different body-part process (ID 0x0431); conditionally used by HEAD::hit.

function_map.name: FREE::2A56
function_map.role: FREE-subsystem routine invoked with code 0x0202; appears to perform cleanup or a state transition for the object before further logic.

function_map.name: Kernel::getNumProcesses
function_map.role: Query number of running processes of a given type for the object; return value is used by HEAD::hit to decide conditional spawning.

external_calls.purpose: Attach info key 0x0207 to the HEAD object to record state/metadata before spawning.
Mark the HEAD object to be excluded or have altered processing immediately after setting info.

Create/start a body-part process associated with the HEAD object; spawn is passed the object's 2-byte indirect field and an explicit 0 argument. The spawn return value is stored into a local temp and returned.
Attach info key 0x020B to the HEAD object to record a hit-related state change.
Mark the HEAD object to be excluded or have altered processing after setting hit info.
Invoke a FREE subsystem routine with code 0x0202, likely for cleanup or a state transition related to the hit event.
Query how many processes of type 0x020A exist for this object; the numeric return is inverted and tested to decide whether to spawn BODYPART::0431.
Conditionally spawn an alternate body-part process tied to the HEAD object if the post-inversion test on getNumProcesses passes.

quick_facts.behavior_summary_one_liner: HEAD::use (Event 1) marks the HEAD object with a specific info key, applies a process-exclude to alter/stop normal processing for that object, and spawns a body-part process (BODYPART::0270) owned by the HEAD object. The spawn return value is captured and returned.
